description_en: A thing which can be added to something else in order to make it more useful, versatile, or attractive.
example: A stylish belt is a great accessory to complete your outfit.

description_en: A thing bought or offered for sale much more cheaply than is usual or expected.
example: I found a real bargain at the end-of-season sale.
note: Both a noun and a verb.

description_en: A group of things that have been collected, especially for study or exhibition.
example: The designer's latest winter collection was inspired by nature.

description_en: A deduction from the usual cost of something.
example: Students are entitled to a 10% discount on all purchases.

description_en: Pleasingly graceful and stylish in appearance or manner.
example: She looked very elegant in her long black evening gown.

description_en: Cloth, typically produced by weaving or knitting textile fibers.
example: This shirt is made from a lightweight and breathable cotton fabric.

description_en: An item of clothing.
example: All leather garments should be cleaned by a specialist.

description_en: Expensive, fashionable clothes produced by leading fashion houses.
example: Haute couture designs are often custom-made for specific clients.

meaning: hàng tồn kho; danh mục hàng hóa
description_en: A complete list of items such as property, goods in stock, or the contents of a building.
example: The store is closed today for its annual inventory check.

description_en: Personal ornaments, such as necklaces, rings, or bracelets, that are typically made from or contain jewels and precious metal.
example: He bought her a beautiful piece of diamond jewelry for their anniversary.

description_en: Made by intertwining yarn or thread in a series of connected loops.
example: I love wearing soft knitted sweaters during the winter.

description_en: The state of great comfort and extravagant living.
example: The hotel offers the ultimate luxury experience for its guests.

description_en: A dummy used to display clothes in a store window.
example: The mannequin in the window was dressed in a stylish red coat.

description_en: The quality of being new, original, or unusual.
example: The store sells a variety of fun novelty items and gifts.

description_en: A set of clothes worn together, typically for a particular occasion or purpose.
example: I need to find a suitable outfit for the wedding next week.

description_en: Acquire (something) by paying for it.
example: You can make your purchase online or in-store.

description_en: The standard of something as measured against other things of a similar kind; the degree of excellence of something.
example: Always check the quality of the fabric before buying expensive clothes.

description_en: The sale of goods to the public in relatively small quantities for use or consumption rather than for resale.
example: The retail industry is facing challenges due to the rise of e-commerce.

description_en: Fashionably elegant and sophisticated.
example: He is one of the Most stylish and well-dressed men I know.
note: Possessing a good sense of style.

description_en: A person whose occupation is making fitted clothes such as suits, pants, and jackets to fit individual customers.
example: I had my suit custom-made by a professional tailor.

description_en: Designed to be useful or practical rather than attractive.
example: Utility boots have a very utilitarian and rugged design.

description_en: Denoting something of high quality, especially something from the past or characteristic of the best period of a person's work.
example: She loves shopping for vintage clothes in second-hand stores.
note: From a past era, often high quality.

meaning: tủ quần áo; bộ sưu tập quần áo cá nhân
description_en: A large, tall cabinet in which clothes may be hung or stored.
example: It's time to update my summer wardrobe with some new shirts and shorts.
note: Collection of clothes a person owns.

description_en: A size of clothing that is smaller than small.
example: This shirt is available in sizes from x-small to x-large.

description_en: A sale that takes place once every year.
example: The department store's yearly sale is always very popular.

description_en: A device used for fastening together two edges of fabric or other flexible material.
example: The zipper on my jacket is stuck and won't move.

description_en: Clothing.
example: The store specializes in athletic apparel and footwear.

description_en: A small store selling fashionable clothes or accessories.
example: She runs a successful luxury boutique in the city center.

description_en: Elegantly and stylishly fashionable.
example: French women are famous for their effortlessly chic style.

description_en: A person who plans the look or workings of something prior to its being made.
example: He is a world-renowned fashion designer.

description_en: Very unusual or remarkable.
example: The designer used extraordinary materials for the gala dress.

description_en: A room in a store where you can put on clothes to see if they fit.
example: The fitting rooms were full, so I had to wait ten minutes.

description_en: Having glamour; excitingly attractive.
example: Red carpet events are always full of glamorous celebrities and fans.

description_en: A small bag used by women for carrying personal items.
example: She bought a luxury leather handbag to go with her new dress.

description_en: An individual article or unit, especially one that is part of a list, collection, or set.
example: This item is currently out of stock but can be pre-ordered.

description_en: Knitted clothing.
example: Woolen knitwear is perfect for staying warm in the cold weather.

description_en: A small piece of paper, fabric, plastic, or similar material attached to an object and giving information about it.
example: Check the care label before washing the silk blouse.

description_en: The matter from which a thing is or can be made.
example: Synthetic materials like polyester are often less expensive than natural ones.

description_en: A narrrow strip of fabric worn around the neck and tied into a knot.
example: He wore a silk necktie for the formal dinner.

description_en: The excess of something.
example: The store had an overflow of stock after the shipment arrived.

description_en: A repeated decorative design.
example: The floral pattern on the summer dress is very pretty.

description_en: The amount or number of a material or immaterial thing not usually estimated by spatial measurement.
example: The store offers a discount for buying in large quantities.

description_en: Sold in standard sizes rather than being made to order for an individual customer.
example: Most of the clothes we buy in malls are ready-to-wear.

description_en: The dark shape and outline of someone or something visible against a lighter background.
example: The designer focused on creating a feminine and elegant silhouette.

description_en: A type of cloth or woven fabric.
example: The textile industry is one of the oldest in the world.

description_en: Clothing worn next to the skin and under other clothes.
example: Make sure to pack enough underwear for the three-day trip.

description_en: A neckline that is shaped like a V.
example: She prefers wearing v-neck sweaters to crew-neck ones.

description_en: The fine, soft curly or wavy hair forming the coat of a sheep.
example: A pure wool coat will keep you very warm in the winter.

description_en: A unit of linear measure equal to 3 feet (0.9144 meters).
example: The dress required five yards of silk fabric.

description_en: A group of five or nine numbers included in a postal address to assist the sorting of mail.
example: You need to enter your zip code for the shipping calculation.
